sourceMovie.title: Jumper
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Jumper (2008) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Spider-Man 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Spider-Man 2 shares Jumper's gift for action — the 2004 film delivers adventure without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Jumper compelling — the action, the adventure — shows up in The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 2 (2015) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that tense storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: The Incredible Hulk
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action energy of Jumper is alive in The Incredible Hulk (2008), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a tense payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Alita: Battle Angel
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Alita: Battle Angel (2019) operates in the same action as Jumper, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The tense payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: I Am Number Four
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: I Am Number Four (2011) belongs to the same conversation as Jumper — both anchor their action in adventure emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: The Flash
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Jumper's action stayed with you, The Flash (2023) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The adventure through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Jumper excels in action, Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga (2024) doubles down on adventure to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Predator: Badlands
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Predator: Badlands (2025) is the natural next step after Jumper: same action DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and adventure elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Secret Headquarters
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Jumper who respond to its action will find Secret Headquarters (2022) immediately familiar. Both films share a adventure sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Wonder Woman 1984
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Jumper fans keep landing on Wonder Woman 1984 (2020) — the action is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

faq.question: What should I watch after Jumper?
faq.answer: Try Spider-Man 2, The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 2, The Incredible Hulk—each shares DNA with Jumper while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Jumper?
faq.answer: Jumper (2008) is best described as Action, Adventure, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Jumper worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
